steps.title: Push. Move. Transfer energy.
steps.body:
You're about to push a block and watch energy move in real time.

**Work** is energy transferred by a force over a distance. No friction? Every joule goes straight into motion. That's the deal:

steps.prompt: You push a block with **10 N** over **2 m**. No friction. Drag the **W_net** bar to show how much work you did.
hints: Work = force × distance when they point the same way.
W_net = 10 N × 2 m = 20 J. Match the bar to that.

steps.prompt: Same push — no friction. By the work-energy theorem, how much did the block's kinetic energy change? Drag **ΔKE**.
hints: W_net = ΔKE. You already found W_net.
If 20 J of work went into the block, ΔKE = 20 J.

steps.title: Lesson complete!
steps.body: You sized work and kinetic energy change — and saw that W_net = ΔKE every time.
